source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In the impedance triangle of an RC series AC circuit shown, it is known that \( X_{\mathrm{C}} = R \) and the initial phase of the circuit current is \( 15^{\circ} \). The incorrect statement is
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: The initial phase of the total voltage in the series circuit is \( 60^{\circ} \)

choice_map.B: The initial phase of the voltage across the resistor is \( 15^{\circ} \)
choice_map.C: The initial phase of the voltage across the capacitor is \( -75^{\circ} \)
choice_map.D: The initial phase of the total voltage in the series circuit is \( 60^{\circ} \)

the options involve different derived quantities (impedance magnitude, resistor voltage phase, capacitor voltage phase, total voltage phase), implying at least two or more reasoning steps
